criteria.description: DashboardData gains a `oneLineGoal` field: the verbatim first meaningful line of state.goal, skipping YAML frontmatter (a leading `---` … `---` block) and a leading Markdown `#` heading (stripping only the leading `#`/`##…` marker + following spaces, keeping the remaining text verbatim). It is never AI-summarized/reworded, and is null when state.goal is absent/empty.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit tests in tests/dashboard-reader.test.ts: on the `complete` fixture (goal has `---` frontmatter then `# Goal: ship the thing`) assert oneLineGoal === 'Goal: ship the thing'; a temp fixture with a plain-text goal returns its first non-empty line verbatim; a temp fixture whose first meaningful line has no heading marker is returned unchanged; a temp fixture with frontmatter followed by a plain-text (non-`#`) first body line returns that body line verbatim (frontmatter skipped, no heading strip); null when goal missing/empty.

criteria.description: DashboardData gains a `repoName` field = basename of state.projectPath when present, else basename of state.runDir, else basename of the runDir path passed to assembleDashboardData; null only when none yields a name.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit tests: on `complete` fixture assert repoName === 'dev-harness' (basename of projectPath /Users/mattli/development/dev-harness); a temp fixture with no projectPath but a runDir asserts basename(runDir); a temp fixture with neither asserts basename of the passed run-folder path.

criteria.description: DashboardData gains a coarse forward-only `runStage` field with one of exactly three values ('plan' | 'generate' | 'done'), derived only from status + plan-presence: 'done' when status is passed/halted; else 'generate' when a plan exists (state.sprints non-empty); else 'plan'. It is a pure function of (status, plan-presence) with no per-sprint segmentation.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit tests covering the mapping truth table: `complete` (passed) → 'done'; `partial` (running, has sprints) → 'generate'; a temp planning fixture (running, empty sprints) → 'plan'; a temp halted fixture (status halted) → 'done'; and a forward-only case — a passed run with EMPTY sprints still → 'done' (a done/terminal status never falls back to 'plan' despite absent plan).

criteria.description: Each SprintSummary row gains `buildAttempts` = count of GENERATE trace events whose sprint matches that row's index, derived purely by counting trace events and NEVER read from state.iterations. The value must equal the true GENERATE count even when state.iterations disagrees. GENERATE events whose `sprint` is missing/non-number are attributed to NO row (never mis-bucketed into sprint 0) and never cause a throw. The ≥2-GENERATE-events richness (and the state.iterations-disagreement and unattributed-GENERATE traps) is exercised on a NEW/DEDICATED fixture (e.g. `metrics`), leaving the `complete` fixture's trace unchanged so c6's sums and existing cost tests stay valid.
criteria.verifyBy: Behavioral test (no source grep) on a new/dedicated fixture (NOT by mutating `complete`): a sprint has ≥2 GENERATE trace events while state.iterations holds a DIFFERENT value (e.g. iterations:5 while GENERATE count ≥2); assert row.buildAttempts equals the counted GENERATE events (the exact number ≥2), NOT state.iterations. Per-index GENERATE counts computed from that fixture's trace.jsonl match each row.buildAttempts. Plus a trap: a GENERATE event with an absent/non-number `sprint` field does not throw and is not counted into row 0's buildAttempts (it contributes to no row). Additionally assert on the untouched `complete` fixture that each row.buildAttempts equals its true GENERATE count (1 per sprint), confirming `complete` was not mutated.

criteria.description: Each SprintSummary row gains `fileEdits` = count of 'Edit' string entries across the toolCalls[] arrays of that sprint's GENERATE events only (only exact 'Edit' matches; only GENERATE events attributed to that sprint index). Non-'Edit' entries (e.g. 'Write') and 'Edit' entries in non-GENERATE events are excluded, as are Edit entries in GENERATE events with a missing/non-number `sprint`. The ≥2-Edit richness and the traps are exercised on the same NEW/DEDICATED fixture as c4, leaving `complete` unchanged.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test on the new/dedicated fixture (NOT `complete`) where a sprint has ≥2 GENERATE events with ≥2 'Edit' entries spread across those multiple GENERATE events (plus non-Edit entries like 'Write'), AND an 'Edit' entry planted in a non-GENERATE event (e.g. an EVALUATE event) as a trap, AND an 'Edit' in a GENERATE event with missing/non-number `sprint` as a second trap: assert row.fileEdits equals the exact total of 'Edit' entries in properly-attributed GENERATE events (≥2), excluding the 'Write' entries, the non-GENERATE 'Edit', and the unattributed-GENERATE 'Edit'.

criteria.description: Each SprintSummary row gains `costUsd` = sum of costUsd across ALL of that sprint's trace events (every phase, not just GENERATE), tolerating missing/non-number costUsd as 0; result is a finite number (never NaN).
criteria.verifyBy: Unit test that, on the `complete` fixture, computes the expected per-sprint cost sum DYNAMICALLY by reading `complete`'s own trace.jsonl (grouping costUsd by sprint index in the test), and asserts equality with each row.costUsd using toBeCloseTo (epsilon, not ===) to avoid IEEE-754 flake — no hardcoded 0.08/0.04, so the assertion survives any fixture edits. Plus a case (temp or dedicated fixture) where an event's costUsd is missing/non-number contributes 0 and the row.costUsd is finite (Number.isFinite true, not NaN).

criteria.description: All new derivations degrade gracefully: on the `corrupt` fixture (unparseable state.json) the reader still returns a non-null object with degraded===true, oneLineGoal===null, runStage==='plan', no throw, and repoName === basename of the runDir path passed to assembleDashboardData (the passed path is threaded into the degraded object); empty/absent trace yields buildAttempts/fileEdits 0 and costUsd 0 per row.
criteria.verifyBy: Unit tests: assembleDashboardData on `corrupt` does not throw and has degraded===true, oneLineGoal===null, runStage==='plan', and repoName === basename(passed runDir path) (single asserted value, not 'or null'); a temp fixture with valid state but empty trace.jsonl yields sprint rows with buildAttempts===0, fileEdits===0, costUsd===0.

criteria.description: The reader module stays import-safe and stdlib-only: it imports only `node:` modules and type-only local `../state/types.js` / `../trace/types.js`; no node:http, no SDK, no cli/orchestrator/agents imports; importing binds no port and starts no server.
criteria.verifyBy: The existing 'module source imports only Node stdlib' test in tests/dashboard-reader.test.ts remains green after edits (extended if new imports are added, still asserting stdlib/type-only).

criteria.description: Existing reader behavior and the full existing suite remain green: all previously-asserted DashboardData fields, sprint breakdown (rounds/score/current/description), scores, elapsedMs freeze/count-up, findLatestRunDir, and graceful-degradation cases are unchanged. The `complete` fixture's trace.jsonl is not mutated by this sprint (new richness lives in a dedicated fixture), so existing cost/round/score assertions keyed to `complete` still pass.
criteria.verifyBy: `npm test` passes with the entire prior tests/dashboard-reader.test.ts and all other suites green.

scope.description: Changes limited to the dashboard module and its tests/fixtures: src/dashboard/* and tests/dashboard-* (plus tests/fixtures/dashboard/*). This sprint focuses on src/dashboard/reader.ts and tests/dashboard-reader.test.ts (and any new/extended dashboard fixtures).

Read-only reader: no writes/creates/modifies of any run-folder or config file; no changes to trace emission, run.ts, state.iterations, or any harness machinery. build attempts are derived from GENERATE events, never from state.iterations.
No new npm dependencies; stdlib-only Node. No server/page (renderPage) changes in this sprint beyond what reader typing requires — the HTTP/page port is a later sprint.

criteria.description: GET / returns 200 text/html and the served page renders a goal-first header where the repo name (basename of state.projectPath, falling back to runDir basename) appears before the run ID, which appears before a status pill whose class/text reflects the run status (planning/running/passed/halted). The repo name must be DERIVED from a fixture's projectPath/runDir basename, not a hardcoded literal.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test: start server on ephemeral port against fixtures, GET /, assert 200 + content-type text/html; assert the repo name derived from a fixture's projectPath basename (e.g. 'dev-harness') and the run ID substrings appear with repo positioned before runId before the status pill in the served HTML (verify by substring index ordering); assert a status pill element carries the status. Cross-check: the asserted repo name equals path.basename(fixture.state.projectPath).

criteria.description: The page renders the verbatim one-line goal (deriveOneLineGoal: first meaningful line with YAML frontmatter and a leading '#' heading stripped, never summarized) prominently on the planning state and as a reachable 'View goal' link on non-planning states, plus a reachable full-goal view rendering the complete state.goal.
criteria.verifyBy:
Fetch-level test against the complete/passed fixture whose state.goal is '---
type: dev-harness-goal
---
# Goal: ship the thing
Build the read-only dashboard.': (a) POSITIVE — assert the one-line/header goal element contains exactly 'Build the read-only dashboard.'; (b) NEGATIVE — assert that same one-line element does NOT contain the frontmatter marker 'type: dev-harness-goal', a bare '---' delimiter, or the '# Goal:' heading text (i.e. frontmatter + heading are stripped, not dumped raw). For the planning fixture assert the one-line goal shows prominently; for a non-planning fixture assert a 'View goal' affordance; assert the complete goal body (including the stripped lines) is reachable/rendered in the full-goal view.

criteria.description: The page renders a coarse, forward-only Plan -> Generate -> Done full-width run strip with exactly those three fixed stage labels (no per-sprint segments), fill/color advancing by outcome: in-progress accent, passed shown green with a 'Done' stage, halted shown amber with a 'Paused' treatment.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test: assert the served HTML contains the three stage labels 'Plan', 'Generate', 'Done' in a run-strip element; assert the passed fixture reflects a completed/green strip and the halted fixture reflects a paused/amber strip (class or state marker); assert no per-sprint segment markup exists in the strip.

criteria.description: Stats tiles (Sprint X / N, Elapsed relabeling to Duration when finished, Spend) render once a plan exists and are ABSENT during planning.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test: for a plan-bearing running fixture assert Sprint/Elapsed/Spend tiles present with 'Elapsed' label and the X / N count; for a finished fixture assert the tile relabels to 'Duration'; for the planning fixture (no plan) assert the stats tiles / their labels are absent from the page.

criteria.description: Each sprint card renders title + score (score when done, 'best N' when halted), the plan description, the Negotiate -> Generate -> Evaluate -> Decide phase pipeline on the active sprint, and a per-sprint metrics sub-line reading '<N> negotiation rounds · <N> build attempts · <N> file edits · $<cost>' using reader-derived values. Build attempts MUST equal the count of GENERATE trace events for that sprint (never state.iterations); file edits MUST equal the count of 'Edit' entries across that sprint's GENERATE toolCalls[]; cost MUST equal the sum of costUsd across that sprint's trace events.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test against a metrics/halted fixture whose active/metrics sprint has state.iterations set to a value DIFFERENT from its GENERATE-event count (e.g. iterations wrong, GENERATE events = 3, Edit toolCalls = 24, summed costUsd known): assert the rendered 'build attempts' number equals the GENERATE-event count (3) and is NOT the state.iterations value; assert 'file edits' equals the Edit toolCalls count (24); assert the '$'-prefixed cost equals the summed costUsd; assert the sub-line contains the words 'negotiation rounds', 'build attempts', 'file edits' (fixtures use N≠1 so plural forms appear); assert the active sprint shows the four phase names Negotiate/Generate/Evaluate/Decide; assert a done sprint shows its score and a halted sprint shows 'best N'.

criteria.description: A HALT_REASONS map covers all StopReason codes (max-iteration, no-progress, dollar-ceiling, wall-clock, usage-limit) as graceful 'Paused' plus evaluator-parse-error as a 'Stopped' fault, with wording matching the prototype's committed HALT_REASONS map verbatim, rendered as a plain-language banner only when the run is halted.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test: for the halted fixture (haltReason max-iteration) assert the served page contains the committed verbatim sentence 'Used all its attempts on this sprint (6 tries) without clearing the score bar. Paused — partial work is saved.' and its 'Paused' label. Unit/string assertion pinning every entry to the prototype's exact text: 'wall-clock'/'no-progress'/'usage-limit'/'dollar-ceiling'/'max-iteration' each carry label 'Paused' with their committed sentences (lines 326-330 of the prototype), and 'evaluator-parse-error' carries label 'Stopped' with verbatim 'The grader returned an unreadable score, so the run stopped to be safe. This is a fault, not a normal pause.' Assert the banner is absent for non-halted fixtures.

criteria.description: All four run states (planning, running/live, passed, halted) render a 200 page selected purely from run-folder data, and the page retains dark mode, responsive layout, a client-side setInterval+fetch('/data') polling loop with in-place DOM updates (no meta-refresh, no full-page reload), and graceful degradation on corrupt state.json.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level tests: a planning fixture, running fixture, passed fixture, and halted fixture each return 200 with their state's structure; assert the served JS contains setInterval and fetch('/data') and a DOM-mutation API, and contains NO http-equiv=refresh and no location.reload/location.assign/location.replace/location.href/window.location; assert prefers-color-scheme dark CSS and a viewport meta tag; assert the corrupt fixture still returns 200 with an updating/placeholder signal. /data still returns 200 JSON.

criteria.description: Fixture run folders under tests/fixtures/dashboard/ cover all four states: a planning fixture (plan/sprints absent), a running fixture, a passed fixture, and a halted fixture carrying a real StopReason haltReason code. The halted (or a dedicated metrics) fixture must have a sprint whose state.iterations value DIFFERS from its number of GENERATE trace events, and whose trace.jsonl contains multiple GENERATE events, multiple 'Edit' entries in toolCalls[], and per-event costUsd. At least one non-planning fixture must carry a projectPath (or runDir) whose basename is the repo name asserted in c1.
criteria.verifyBy: Inspect fixtures: confirm the four states; confirm the halted/metrics fixture's sprint has state.iterations != GENERATE-event count, a trace.jsonl with multiple GENERATE events, 'Edit' toolCalls entries, and per-event costUsd, and a StopReason haltReason; confirm a non-planning fixture's projectPath basename equals the c1-asserted repo name. npm test consumes them in the fetch-level assertions above.

criteria.description: The dashboard module stays import-safe and stdlib-only: importing src/dashboard/server.ts binds no port and starts no server, and it imports only Node stdlib plus the sibling reader (and type-only local types); the full existing vitest suite remains green.
criteria.verifyBy: Run npm test: existing import-safety and SDK-free import-closure tests pass; assert no top-level start()/listen(); the complete existing suite (reader, server, page tests) passes offline with no network and no new npm dependencies.

scope.description: Only src/dashboard/* and tests/dashboard-* (plus tests/fixtures/dashboard/*) may change. No changes outside the dashboard module and its tests.
No changes to trace emission, run.ts, or any harness machinery; in particular do not touch or fix state.iterations — derive build attempts from GENERATE events.

Server stays stdlib-only Node (built-in http/fs/path), polling fetch, strictly read-only — never writes/creates/modifies any run-folder or config file; no launch/stop controls, no config editing. No new npm dependencies; a stdlib-only markdown/goal renderer is fine (do not add a markdown library).

criteria.description: A 'planning' fixture run folder exists at tests/fixtures/dashboard/planning/ with state.json (goal present, status a pre-plan/planning state, empty or absent sprints[] so no plan has formed yet) and a trace.jsonl (or none) consistent with 'plan not yet formed'. Its goal carries YAML frontmatter + a leading '#' heading so the verbatim one-line goal derivation is exercised.
criteria.verifyBy: Read the fixture files in a test; assert state.json parses and sprints is empty/absent. Assert GET /data for it reports totalSprints===0 and an empty sprintBreakdown, and that the served page's run strip renders at the Plan stage. npm test.

criteria.description: The halted fixture (tests/fixtures/dashboard/halted/) carries a real StopReason haltReason code and trace events exercising the per-sprint metrics: multiple GENERATE events for the sprint, 'Edit' entries inside toolCalls[] of GENERATE events, and per-event costUsd, plus a best-so-far score (no final passing score). The served halted page renders that score as 'best N' wording (best-so-far, per the prototype), distinct from a done card's plain score. live (running) and passed states remain covered by existing partial/complete fixtures.
criteria.verifyBy: Assert halted state.haltReason is a known StopReason code; assert its trace has >=2 GENERATE events and >=1 'Edit' in a GENERATE event's toolCalls. Assert /data sprintBreakdown for that sprint reports the reader's real derived fields attempts>=2, edits>=1, cost>0, and a non-null best-so-far score (sprintBreakdown[i].score, populated from bestScoreBySprint). ALSO assert at the fetch level that the served halted page contains 'best N' wording rendering that best-so-far score (e.g. text matching /best\s+<score>/), and that a passed/done card renders a plain score without the 'best' prefix. npm test.

criteria.description: GET / returns 200 with correct per-state structural elements for ALL FOUR states served from their fixtures — planning, live(running), passed, halted. planning renders the prominent verbatim one-line goal (with a goal eyebrow/label) and OMITS the stats tiles (no Sprint X/N, Elapsed/Duration, Spend tiles). live/passed render the stats tiles. All non-planning states render a small 'View goal' link.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level tests: for planning assert the one-line goal text appears prominently and tile labels are absent; for live+passed assert tile labels ('Sprint'/'Elapsed'|'Duration'/'Spend') present; for non-planning assert a 'View goal' link/affordance present. npm test.

criteria.description: The run strip renders the coarse monotonic Plan -> Generate -> Done stages full-width under the goal-first header (repo basename, run id, status pill) for every state, with outcome coloring/state classes: in-progress accent for planning/running, 'Done'/green for passed, 'Paused'/amber for halted. No per-sprint segments.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test asserts served HTML for each state contains the three stage labels Plan/Generate/Done, the repo basename + runId + a status pill, and a halted/passed distinguishing marker (e.g. 'Paused' text/class for halted, done/green for passed). npm test.

criteria.description: A reachable full-goal view renders the COMPLETE state.goal via a stdlib-only markdown/goal renderer (no markdown library, no new npm deps): headings and lists rendered as HTML (not a raw monospace dump), and the full goal body text present. It is reachable either as its own route (e.g. GET /goal) returning 200 HTML, or an in-page view; the 'View goal' affordance targets it.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test hits the full-goal route (or asserts the in-page full-goal container) and asserts the complete goal body text is present and rendered (e.g. a '# Heading' becomes an <h1>/<hN> and a '- item' becomes an <li>), served with 200. Assert package.json gained no new dependency. npm test.

criteria.description: A plain-language halt-reason banner renders on the halted fixture translating the raw code to the exact prototype HALT_REASONS wording, and the mapping covers ALL StopReason codes (wall-clock, max-iteration, no-progress, usage-limit, dollar-ceiling) as 'Paused' plus evaluator-parse-error as 'Stopped'. Non-halted states show no banner.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test: halted fixture's served page contains the exact HALT_REASONS sentence for its code and the 'Paused'/'Stopped' label; a unit/source test asserts all six codes map to the exact prototype text with correct Paused/Stopped labels; passed/running/planning pages contain no halt banner. npm test.

criteria.description: The per-sprint quiet metrics sub-line renders on the sprint cards reading exactly '<N> negotiation rounds · <N> build attempts · <N> file edits · $<cost>' (build attempts derived by counting GENERATE events, file edits by counting 'Edit' toolCalls, cost summed costUsd), with the words 'negotiation rounds', 'build attempts', and 'file edits' present verbatim on the served page for a fixture that has them.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level test asserts the served page for the halted (and/or complete) fixture contains 'negotiation rounds', 'build attempts', 'file edits' and the derived counts/cost. npm test.

criteria.description: /data still returns 200 application/json for all four states and the served page still polls via a fetch('/data') + setInterval loop with in-place DOM updates (getElementById/textContent/innerHTML) and NO meta-refresh and no full-page reload; the reader still degrades gracefully (corrupt fixture → 200, stale:true, no throw). Dark mode + mobile-responsive layout retained.
criteria.verifyBy: Fetch-level tests: /data 200 JSON for planning/live/passed/halted; served HTML matches setInterval + fetch('/data') and a DOM-mutation API, and does NOT match http-equiv=refresh or location.reload/window.location; corrupt fixture /data 200 stale:true. npm test.

criteria.description: The module stays import-safe: importing src/dashboard/server.ts (and reader.ts) binds no port and starts no server, and imports only Node stdlib + the sibling reader + type-only local types (no Anthropic SDK / orchestrator / agents / cli). The full existing test suite remains green.
criteria.verifyBy: Existing import-safety/import-closure tests pass unchanged; run the entire suite: npm test is fully green.

scope.description: Only src/dashboard/* and tests/dashboard-* (plus tests/fixtures/dashboard/*) may change. No changes outside the dashboard module and its tests/fixtures.

No changes to trace emission, run.ts, or any harness machinery; in particular do NOT touch or fix state.iterations — derive build attempts from GENERATE events. The server stays stdlib-only Node, polling-fetch, and strictly read-only (never writes/creates/modifies any run-folder or config file; no launch/stop/config controls).
No new npm dependencies; the full-goal markdown/goal renderer must be stdlib-only (no markdown library added).
